result.stdout:
SpecSync for Jira v5.3.0 (02/04/2026)
Copyright © 2016-2026 Spec Solutions

Description:
  Synchronization tool for integrating BDD scenarios with Jira

Usage:
  specsync [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  init                                Initializes SpecSync configuration
  upgrade                             Upgrades SpecSync configuration
  push <CONFIG_FILE>                  Push changes of the scenarios on the local repository to the Jira server. This by default includes linking of new scenarios to new test cases (link) and updating test cases of linked scenarios (update).
  pull <CONFIG_FILE>                  Pull changes from Jira server to the local repository. This by default includes creation of new scenarios from unlinked test cases (create) and changing scenarios of linked test cases (change).
  publish-test-results <CONFIG_FILE>  Publish local test results to Jira server.
  re-link <CONFIG_FILE>               Re-link scenarios (local test cases) to cloned Test Cases in Jira.
  info <CONFIG_FILE>                  Display configuration and license information.

SpecSync for Jira v5.3.0 (02/04/2026)
Copyright © 2016-2026 Spec Solutions

Description:
  Synchronization tool for integrating BDD scenarios with Jira

Usage:
  specsync [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  init                                Initializes SpecSync configuration
  upgrade                             Upgrades SpecSync configuration
  push <CONFIG_FILE>                  Push changes of the scenarios on the local repository to the Jira server. This by default includes linking of new scenarios to new test cases (link) and updating test cases of linked scenarios (update).
  pull <CONFIG_FILE>                  Pull changes from Jira server to the local repository. This by default includes creation of new scenarios from unlinked test cases (create) and changing scenarios of linked test cases (change).
  publish-test-results <CONFIG_FILE>  Publish local test results to Jira server.
  re-link <CONFIG_FILE>               Re-link scenarios (local test cases) to cloned Test Cases in Jira.
  info <CONFIG_FILE>                  Display configuration and license information.

result.stdout:
SpecSync for Jira v5.3.0 (02/04/2026)
Copyright © 2016-2026 Spec Solutions

Description:
  Display configuration and license information.

Usage:
  specsync info [<CONFIG_FILE>] [options]

Arguments:
  <CONFIG_FILE>  The path of the SpecSync for Jira configuration file, absolute path or relative to the current folder, e.g. 'MyProject.Specs\specsync4tfs.json'. [default: specsync.json]

Options:
  --license <LICENSE_FILE>          The license key or the path to the license file (can be relative to the project folder). [default: use from config file or 'specsync.lic']
  --disableStats                    If specified, SpecSync will not collect anonymous error diagnostics and statistics. [default: not specified]
  --log <LOG_FILE>                  If specified, the output will also be saved to a log file. [default: no log file written]
  --configOverride <CONFIG_UPDATE>  Can be used to override configuration file settings. This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ';'. See https://speclink.me/specsync-configoverride for details.
  -v, --verbose                     If specified, diagnostic information will be added to the output. [default: not specified]
  --zeroExitCodeForWarnings         If specified, the command line tool will terminate with 0 exit code even in case of warnings. [default: not specified]
  --forceExitCode                   If specified, the command line tool will terminate with non zero exit code for errors and warnings when executed on Jira pipeline. [default: not specified]
  --treatWarningsAsErrors           If specified, the warnings will be treated as errors. [default: not specified]
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
SpecSync for Jira v5.3.0 (02/04/2026)
Copyright © 2016-2026 Spec Solutions

Description:
  Initializes SpecSync configuration

Usage:
  specsync init [options]

Options:
  -p, --projectUrl <PROJECT_URL>                       The project URL of the Jira project. [default: interactive prompt]
  -c, --config <CONFIG_FILE>                           The full path or the folder of the configuration file being created. [default: 'specsync.json' file in the current folder]
  --ignoreCertificateErrorsForThumbprint <THUMBPRINT>  The thumbprint of the server certificate that should be treated as trusted. It is recommended to install trusted certificates on the operating system instead of using this setting. See https://speclink.me/specsync-cert-error for details. [default: SSL is verified by the OS]
  -v, --verbose                                        If specified, diagnostic information will be added to the output. [default: not specified]
  --zeroExitCodeForWarnings                            If specified, the command line tool will terminate with 0 exit code even in case of warnings. [default: not specified]
  --forceExitCode                                      If specified, the command line tool will terminate with non zero exit code for errors and warnings when executed on Jira pipeline. [default: not specified]
  --treatWarningsAsErrors                              If specified, the warnings will be treated as errors. [default: not specified]
  -?, -h, --help                                       Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
SpecSync for Jira v5.3.0 (02/04/2026)
Copyright © 2016-2026 Spec Solutions

Description:
  Publish local test results to Jira server.

Usage:
  specsync publish-test-results [<CONFIG_FILE>] [options]

Arguments:
  <CONFIG_FILE>  The path of the SpecSync for Jira configuration file, absolute path or relative to the current folder, e.g. 'MyProject.Specs\specsync4tfs.json'. [default: specsync.json]

Options:
  -r, --testResult, --testResultFile <TEST_RESULT_FILE>    The file path of the test result (.trx, .xml or .json) file to publish. This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ';'. [default: use from config file]
  -f, --testResultFileFormat, --testResultFormat <FORMAT>  The file format of the file to publish (use '?' to list possible formats). [default: use from config file or detect automatically]
  --attachedFiles, --attachFile <FILE>                     Semicolon separated list of file paths that should be attached to the test run additionally. (e.g. 'error1.png;error2.log') This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ';'. [default: only the test result file is attached]
  --runName <RUN_NAME>                                     The name of the test run to be created. Can also be used as '--testRunSetting "name=value"'. [default: get from test result file]
  --runComment <COMMENT>                                   The comment or description field of the test run to be created. Can also be used as '--testRunSetting "comment=value"'. [default: get from test result file]
  --testResultComment <COMMENT>                            The comment added to the individual test results within the created test run. Can also be used as '--testResultSetting "comment=value"'. [default: not specified]
  --testRunSetting <SETTING>                               Can be used to specify additional settings for the test run to be created. For Zephyr Scale Test Cycles you can specify 'name', 'description', 'iteration', 'version' and 'folder'. This option can be used multiple times using the 'setting=value' format, e.g. --testRunSetting "iteration=Iteration 1". This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ';'.
  --testResultSetting <SETTING>                            Can be used to specify additional settings for the individual test results. For Zephyr Scale test results you can specify 'comment' and 'environment'. This option can be used multiple times using the 'setting=value' format, e.g. --testResultSetting "environment=Chrome". This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ';'.
  --filter, --tagFilter <CONDITION>                        A local test case condition of local test cases that should be included in the current operation (e.g. '@current_sprint and @done' or '$name = "My scenario"'). See https://speclink.me/specsync-conditions for details. [default: not filtered]
  --sourceFileFilter <CONDITION>                           An expression of source file patterns that should be included in the current operation (e.g. 'Folder1/**/*.feature'). [default: not filtered by source file]
  --user <USER_NAME>                                       The Jira user name or '?' to force interactive prompt. [default: use from config file or interactive prompt]
  --password <PASSWORD>                                    The password for the Jira user. [default: use from config file or interactive prompt]
  --ignoreCertificateErrorsForThumbprint <T>               The thumbprint of the server certificate that should be treated as trusted. It is recommended to install trusted certificates on the operating system instead of using this setting. See https://speclink.me/specsync-cert-error for details. [default: SSL is verified by the OS]
  --dryRun                                                 If specified, the command will be performed, but no actual change is made either to Jira or to the local test files (feature files). [default: not specified]
  --license <LICENSE_FILE>                                 The license key or the path to the license file (can be relative to the project folder). [default: use from config file or 'specsync.lic']
  --disableStats                                           If specified, SpecSync will not collect anonymous error diagnostics and statistics. [default: not specified]
  --log <LOG_FILE>                                         If specified, the output will also be saved to a log file. [default: no log file written]
  --configOverride <CONFIG_UPDATE>                         Can be used to override configuration file settings. This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ';'. See https://speclink.me/specsync-configoverride for details.
  -v, --verbose                                            If specified, diagnostic information will be added to the output. [default: not specified]
  --zeroExitCodeForWarnings                                If specified, the command line tool will terminate with 0 exit code even in case of warnings. [default: not specified]
  --forceExitCode                                          If specified, the command line tool will terminate with non zero exit code for errors and warnings when executed on Jira pipeline. [default: not specified]
  --treatWarningsAsErrors                                  If specified, the warnings will be treated as errors. [default: not specified]
  -?, -h, --help                                           Show help and usage information

  --user <USER_NAME>                                       The Jira user name or '?' to force interactive prompt. [default: use from config file or interactive prompt]
  --password <PASSWORD>                                    The password for the Jira user. [default: use from config file or interactive prompt]
  --ignoreCertificateErrorsForThumbprint <T>               The thumbprint of the server certificate that should be treated as trusted. It is recommended to install trusted certificates on the operating system instead of using this setting. See https://speclink.me/specsync-cert-error for details. [default: SSL is verified by the OS]
  --dryRun                                                 If specified, the command will be performed, but no actual change is made either to Jira or to the local test files (feature files). [default: not specified]
  --license <LICENSE_FILE>                                 The license key or the path to the license file (can be relative to the project folder). [default: use from config file or 'specsync.lic']
  --disableStats                                           If specified, SpecSync will not collect anonymous error diagnostics and statistics. [default: not specified]
  --log <LOG_FILE>                                         If specified, the output will also be saved to a log file. [default: no log file written]
  --configOverride <CONFIG_UPDATE>                         Can be used to override configuration file settings. This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ';'. See https://speclink.me/specsync-configoverride for details.
  -v, --verbose                                            If specified, diagnostic information will be added to the output. [default: not specified]
  --zeroExitCodeForWarnings                                If specified, the command line tool will terminate with 0 exit code even in case of warnings. [default: not specified]
  --forceExitCode                                          If specified, the command line tool will terminate with non zero exit code for errors and warnings when executed on Jira pipeline. [default: not specified]
  --treatWarningsAsErrors                                  If specified, the warnings will be treated as errors. [default: not specified]
  -?, -h, --help                                           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
SpecSync for Jira v5.3.0 (02/04/2026)
Copyright © 2016-2026 Spec Solutions

Description:
  Pull changes from Jira server to the local repository. This by default includes creation of new scenarios from unlinked test cases (create) and changing scenarios of linked test cases (change).

Usage:
  specsync pull [<CONFIG_FILE>] [options]

Arguments:
  <CONFIG_FILE>  The path of the SpecSync for Jira configuration file, absolute path or relative to the current folder, e.g. 'MyProject.Specs\specsync4tfs.json'. [default: specsync.json]

Options:
  --createOnly                                If specified, the command will create new scenarios for the unlinked Test Cases only and the existing scenarios will not be modified. This setting automatically enables the 'enableCreatingNewLocalTestCases' pull setting. [default: not specified]
  --disableLocalChanges                       If specified, only those changes will be performed that do not need any change in the local feature file. Linking new scenarios or pulling changes from Jira are skipped. [default: not specified]
  --force                                     If specified, SpecSync update test cases even if there is no local change and the test case was not modified remotely. [default: not specified]
  --filter, --tagFilter <CONDITION>           A local test case condition of local test cases that should be included in the current operation (e.g. '@current_sprint and @done' or '$name = "My scenario"'). See https://speclink.me/specsync-conditions for details. [default: not filtered]
  --sourceFileFilter <CONDITION>              An expression of source file patterns that should be included in the current operation (e.g. 'Folder1/**/*.feature'). [default: not filtered by source file]
  --user <USER_NAME>                          The Jira user name or '?' to force interactive prompt. [default: use from config file or interactive prompt]
  --password <PASSWORD>                       The password for the Jira user. [default: use from config file or interactive prompt]
  --ignoreCertificateErrorsForThumbprint <T>  The thumbprint of the server certificate that should be treated as trusted. It is recommended to install trusted certificates on the operating system instead of using this setting. See https://speclink.me/specsync-cert-error for details. [default: SSL is verified by the OS]
  --dryRun                                    If specified, the command will be performed, but no actual change is made either to Jira or to the local test files (feature files). [default: not specified]
  --license <LICENSE_FILE>                    The license key or the path to the license file (can be relative to the project folder). [default: use from config file or 'specsync.lic']
  --disableStats                              If specified, SpecSync will not collect anonymous error diagnostics and statistics. [default: not specified]
  --log <LOG_FILE>                            If specified, the output will also be saved to a log file. [default: no log file written]
  --configOverride <CONFIG_UPDATE>            Can be used to override configuration file settings. This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ';'. See https://speclink.me/specsync-configoverride for details.
  -v, --verbose                               If specified, diagnostic information will be added to the output. [default: not specified]
  --zeroExitCodeForWarnings                   If specified, the command line tool will terminate with 0 exit code even in case of warnings. [default: not specified]
  --forceExitCode                             If specified, the command line tool will terminate with non zero exit code for errors and warnings when executed on Jira pipeline. [default: not specified]
  --treatWarningsAsErrors                     If specified, the warnings will be treated as errors. [default: not specified]
  -?, -h, --help                              Show help and usage information

  --force                                     If specified, SpecSync update test cases even if there is no local change and the test case was not modified remotely. [default: not specified]

  --ignoreCertificateErrorsForThumbprint <T>  The thumbprint of the server certificate that should be treated as trusted. It is recommended to install trusted certificates on the operating system instead of using this setting. See https://speclink.me/specsync-cert-error for details. [default: SSL is verified by the OS]

  --treatWarningsAsErrors                     If specified, the warnings will be treated as errors. [default: not specified]

commands.payload:
SpecSync for Jira v5.3.0 (02/04/2026)
Copyright © 2016-2026 Spec Solutions

Description:
  Push changes of the scenarios on the local repository to the Jira server. This by default includes linking of new scenarios to new test cases (link) and updating test cases of linked scenarios (update).

Usage:
  specsync push [<CONFIG_FILE>] [options]

Arguments:
  <CONFIG_FILE>  The path of the SpecSync for Jira configuration file, absolute path or relative to the current folder, e.g. 'MyProject.Specs\specsync4tfs.json'. [default: specsync.json]

  --linkOnly                                  If specified, the command will link new scenarios to new Test Cases only and the existing Test Cases will not be updated. [default: not specified]
  --disableLocalChanges                       If specified, only those changes will be performed that do not need any change in the local feature file. Linking new scenarios or pulling changes from Jira are skipped. [default: not specified]

It is recommended to install trusted certificates on the operating system instead of using this setting. See https://speclink.me/specsync-cert-error for details. [default: SSL is verified by the OS]
  --dryRun                                    If specified, the command will be performed, but no actual change is made either to Jira or to the local test files (feature files). [default: not specified]
  --license <LICENSE_FILE>                    The license key or the path to the license file (can be relative to the project folder). [default: use from config file or 'specsync.lic']
  --disableStats                              If specified, SpecSync will not collect anonymous error diagnostics and statistics. [default: not specified]
  --log <LOG_FILE>                            If specified, the output will also be saved to a log file. [default: no log file written]
  --configOverride <CONFIG_UPDATE>            Can be used to override configuration file settings. This option can be used multiple times or as one option separated by ';'. See https://speclink.me/specsync-configoverride for details.
  -v, --verbose                               If specified, diagnostic information will be added to the output. [default: not specified]
  --zeroExitCodeForWarnings                   If specified, the command line tool will terminate with 0 exit code even in case of warnings. [default: not specified]
  --forceExitCode                             If specified, the command line tool will terminate with non zero exit code for errors and warnings when executed on Jira pipeline. [default: not specified]
  --treatWarningsAsErrors                     If specified, the warnings will be treated as errors. [default: not specified]
  -?, -h, --help                              Show help and usage information

  --user <USER_NAME>                          The Jira user name or '?' to force interactive prompt. [default: use from config file or interactive prompt]

Description:

Usage:

Options:
